label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why small pieces of space junk can threaten satellites. The options are A through D. First, I need to recall the information from the article. The article mentions that even small debris like paint dots can damage shuttles because they orbit at high speeds, up to 28,200 km/h. The high speed causes damage, so even small pieces can be harmful. Looking at the options, option A says they travel at a very high speed. That matches what the article states.
